rationale: The agent correctly read the required files (limiter.py, bucket.lua, __init__.py), accurately explained the Limiter.check() implementation: key construction with hash-tag, EVALSHA invocation with capacity/refill/cost arguments, Lua script flow (TIME command for server clock, HMGET for state, nil-initialization, float-based token refill capped at capacity, cost consumption logic, HMSET persistence, EXPIRE with calculated TTL), and correctly identified the (bool, int) return value and Redis side effects. The explanation properly identifies this as a token-bucket algorithm, notes tokens are internally floats (floored on return), and does not paste unrelated configs. All expected behaviors are satisfied; none of the listed failure conditions are present.
